rules.summary: Use “Dag” in prose, preserving literal code-token spellings.
rules.trigger_intent: editing prose that refers to Airflow Dags
rules.evidence: Write **Dag** (title case) in all prose.

rules.summary: Do not spell out Directed Acyclic Graph except in historical context.
rules.trigger_intent: writing prose that might expand the Dag acronym
rules.evidence: Don't spell out **Directed Acyclic Graph** except for historical context.

rules.summary: Install prek with uv.
rules.trigger_intent: setting up the development environment
rules.evidence: Install prek: `uv tool install prek`

rules.summary: Enable commit hooks with prek install.
rules.trigger_intent: setting up commit hooks
rules.evidence: Enable commit hooks: `prek install`

rules.summary: Install the Breeze shim using the repository setup script.
rules.trigger_intent: installing Breeze on a development machine
rules.evidence: Install breeze shim (one-time, per machine): `scripts/tools/setup_breeze`

rules.summary: Never run pytest, Python, or Airflow commands directly on the host; use Breeze.
rules.trigger_intent: running pytest, python, or airflow commands
rules.evidence: **Never run pytest, python, or airflow commands directly on the host** — always use `breeze`.

rules.summary: Place temporary scripts under dev/.
rules.trigger_intent: creating a temporary script
rules.evidence: Place temporary scripts in `dev/`

rules.summary: Run a single test with the documented uv project pytest command.
rules.trigger_intent: running one pytest test method
rules.evidence: `uv run --project <PROJECT> pytest path/to/test.py::TestClass::test_method -xvs`

rules.summary: Run a test file with the documented uv project pytest command.
rules.trigger_intent: running one pytest test file
rules.evidence: `uv run --project <PROJECT> pytest path/to/test.py -xvs`

rules.summary: Run all tests in a package with the documented uv project command.
rules.trigger_intent: running all pytest tests in a package
rules.evidence: `uv run --project <PROJECT> pytest path/to/package -xvs`

rules.summary: If uv tests lack system dependencies, rerun them with Breeze.
rules.trigger_intent: rerunning uv tests after missing-system-dependency failures
rules.evidence: **If uv tests fail with missing system dependencies, run the tests with breeze**

rules.summary: Run Python scripts with uv and the relevant project.
rules.trigger_intent: running a Python script in the repository
rules.evidence: `uv run --project <PROJECT> python dev/my_script.py`

rules.summary: Run core or provider test suites in parallel with Breeze.
rules.trigger_intent: running the core or providers test suite in parallel
rules.evidence: `breeze testing <test_group> --run-in-parallel`

rules.summary: Use the db-only parallel Breeze command for core/provider database suites.
rules.trigger_intent: running only database tests for core or providers in parallel
rules.evidence: `breeze testing <test_group> --run-db-tests-only --run-in-parallel`

rules.summary: Use the skip-db xdist Breeze command for core/provider non-database suites.
rules.trigger_intent: running only non-database tests for core or providers in parallel
rules.evidence: `breeze testing <test_group> --skip-db-tests --use-xdist`

rules.summary: Use Providers[...] test type for a complete provider suite.
rules.trigger_intent: running a complete test suite for one or more providers
rules.evidence: `breeze testing providers-tests --test-type "Providers[PROVIDERS_LIST]"`

rules.summary: Run Helm tests in parallel with xdist.
rules.trigger_intent: running Helm tests
rules.evidence: `breeze testing helm-tests --use-xdist`

rules.summary: Pass kubernetes-version when running Helm tests for a specific Kubernetes version.
rules.trigger_intent: running Helm tests for a specific Kubernetes version
rules.evidence: `breeze testing helm-tests --use-xdist --kubernetes-version 1.35.0`

rules.summary: Pass test-type when running a specific Helm test category.
rules.trigger_intent: running a specific Helm test type
rules.evidence: `breeze testing helm-tests --use-xdist --test-type <type>`

rules.summary: Run other named test suites with breeze testing.
rules.trigger_intent: running airflow-ctl, docker-compose, or task-sdk test suites
rules.evidence: `breeze testing <test_group>`

rules.summary: Run scripts tests using the scripts uv project.
rules.trigger_intent: running tests for repository scripts
rules.evidence: `uv run --project scripts pytest scripts/tests/ -xvs`

rules.summary: Run the Airflow CLI through Breeze.
rules.trigger_intent: running an Airflow CLI command
rules.evidence: `breeze run airflow dags list`

rules.summary: Type-check non-provider projects through their prek mypy hook.
rules.trigger_intent: type-checking a non-provider project
rules.evidence: `prek run mypy-<project> --all-files`

rules.summary: Type-check providers with Breeze mypy.
rules.trigger_intent: type-checking provider code
rules.evidence: `breeze run mypy path/to/code`

rules.summary: Run ruff lint through prek from the target branch.
rules.trigger_intent: linting changed files with ruff only
rules.evidence: `prek run ruff --from-ref <target_branch>`

rules.summary: Run ruff formatting through prek from the target branch.
rules.trigger_intent: formatting changed files with ruff only
rules.evidence: `prek run ruff-format --from-ref <target_branch>`

rules.summary: Run fast static checks through the pre-commit stage.
rules.trigger_intent: running regular static checks
rules.evidence: `prek run --from-ref <target_branch> --stage pre-commit`

rules.summary: Run slower checks through the manual stage.
rules.trigger_intent: running manual static checks
rules.evidence: `prek run --from-ref <target_branch> --stage manual`

rules.summary: Build documentation with Breeze.
rules.trigger_intent: building project documentation
rules.evidence: `breeze build-docs`

rules.summary: Use selective-checks to determine tests from changed files.
rules.trigger_intent: determining which tests changed files require
rules.evidence: `breeze selective-checks --commit-ref <commit_with_squashed_changes>`

rules.summary: Select postgres or mysql backend for integration tests needing those databases.
rules.trigger_intent: running an integration test that needs PostgreSQL or MySQL
rules.evidence: Use `--backend postgres` or `--backend mysql` for integration tests that need those databases.

rules.summary: Prune Docker networks if Docker networking fails.
rules.trigger_intent: recovering from Docker networking failure
rules.evidence: If Docker networking fails, run `docker network prune`.

rules.summary: Regenerate a conflicted uv.lock by deleting it and running uv lock.
rules.trigger_intent: resolving a merge conflict in uv.lock
rules.evidence: simply delete it and run `uv lock` to regenerate it

rules.summary: Do not make the scheduler execute user code.
rules.trigger_intent: editing scheduler execution behavior
rules.evidence: Scheduler reads serialized Dags — **never runs user code**

rules.summary: Workers must communicate through the Execution API and never access the metadata DB directly.
rules.trigger_intent: editing worker database or API access
rules.evidence: **never access the metadata DB directly**

rules.summary: Declare each distribution's required peer distributions.
rules.trigger_intent: editing a distribution's dependency declarations
rules.evidence: Each of the distributions should declare other needed distributions

rules.summary: Do not report documented security-model limitations as new vulnerabilities.
rules.trigger_intent: writing a security finding or report
rules.evidence: should not be reported as new findings

rules.summary: Put deployment hardening opportunities in deployment guidance, not code-level findings.
rules.trigger_intent: writing about deployment hardening during security review
rules.evidence: These belong in deployment guidance, not as code-level issues.

rules.summary: Immediately format and fix-check every created or modified Python file with ruff.
rules.trigger_intent: finishing creation or modification of a Python file
rules.evidence: Always format and check Python files with ruff immediately after writing or editing them

rules.summary: Do not use assert in production code.
rules.trigger_intent: editing production code that uses assertions
rules.evidence: No `assert` in production code.

rules.summary: Add comments only for non-obvious reasoning and avoid narrating or duplicated comments.
rules.trigger_intent: adding or editing a code comment
rules.evidence: Add a comment only when the reasoning is non-obvious

rules.summary: Use time.monotonic, not time.time, for durations.
rules.trigger_intent: implementing elapsed-time or duration measurement
rules.evidence: `time.monotonic()` for durations, not `time.time()`.

rules.summary: In airflow-core, session-parameter functions must not commit and session must be keyword-only.
rules.trigger_intent: editing an airflow-core function with a session parameter
rules.evidence: functions with a `session` parameter must not call `session.commit()`. Use keyword-only `session` parameters.

rules.summary: Keep imports at the top except for circular imports, worker-isolation lazy loading, or TYPE_CHECKING.
rules.trigger_intent: adding or moving a Python import
rules.evidence: Imports at top of file. Valid exceptions: circular imports, lazy loading for worker isolation, `TYPE_CHECKING` blocks.

rules.summary: Guard heavy type-only imports with TYPE_CHECKING in multiprocess paths.
rules.trigger_intent: adding a heavy type-only import in multi-process code
rules.evidence: Guard heavy type-only imports (e.g., `kubernetes.client`) with `TYPE_CHECKING`

rules.summary: Raise specific built-in or dedicated exceptions, never add direct AirflowException raises.
rules.trigger_intent: adding or modifying exception-raising code
rules.evidence: **Never add new direct `raise AirflowException(...)` usages**

rules.summary: Translate domain errors to correct HTTPException statuses at FastAPI route boundaries.
rules.trigger_intent: editing an airflow-core core API route handler
rules.evidence: Translate domain-layer exceptions to `HTTPException` at FastAPI route boundaries.

rules.summary: Batch synchronous-loop bulk writes with limits and commits, and index cleanup filters.
rules.trigger_intent: adding bulk DELETE or UPDATE in a scheduler loop or synchronous interval task
rules.evidence: must be batched with `LIMIT` and committed between batches

rules.summary: Name functions and methods with action verbs, except is_/has_ predicates.
rules.trigger_intent: naming or renaming a function or method
rules.evidence: Name functions and methods with action verbs

rules.summary: Add an Apache License header to every new file.
rules.trigger_intent: creating any new repository file
rules.evidence: Apache License header on all new files

rules.summary: When selective-checks rules change, update its documentation and tests in the same PR.
rules.trigger_intent: changing selective_checks.py decision logic
rules.evidence: update `dev/breeze/doc/ci/04_selective_checks.md` in the same PR

rules.summary: Create newsfragments only for certainly user-facing major or breaking changes in consuming distributions.
rules.trigger_intent: creating a newsfragment
rules.evidence: never create a newsfragment unless you are certain the change is user-facing

rules.summary: Never create provider or airflow-ctl newsfragments; update their changelogs directly.
rules.trigger_intent: creating a newsfragment for providers or airflow-ctl
rules.evidence: **Never add newsfragments for `providers/` or `airflow-ctl/`**

rules.summary: Use airflow-core newsfragments for task-sdk changes.
rules.trigger_intent: creating a user-visible note for task-sdk changes
rules.evidence: Changes to `task-sdk/` ship in `airflow-core` — use `airflow-core/newsfragments/`.

rules.summary: Tests must cover exactly changed behavior and fail without the change.
rules.trigger_intent: creating tests for a pull request change
rules.evidence: Every changed or added behaviour must have a test; every test must fail without the PR's change.

rules.summary: Use pytest patterns instead of unittest.TestCase.
rules.trigger_intent: writing or modifying Python tests
rules.evidence: Use pytest patterns, not `unittest.TestCase`.

rules.summary: Use spec or autospec for mocks.
rules.trigger_intent: creating a mock in a test
rules.evidence: Use `spec`/`autospec` when mocking.

rules.summary: Prefer patch decorators and use conf_vars appropriately for config overrides.
rules.trigger_intent: patching dependencies or overriding Airflow configuration in tests
rules.evidence: Prefer `@mock.patch` decorators over `with mock.patch(...)` context managers

rules.summary: Use time_machine, never datetime.now, in time-dependent tests.
rules.trigger_intent: writing a time-dependent test
rules.evidence: Use `time_machine` for time-dependent tests. Do not use `datetime.now()`

rules.summary: Parametrize tests that differ only by input and expected output.
rules.trigger_intent: writing multiple tests for similar input cases
rules.evidence: Use `@pytest.mark.parametrize` for multiple similar inputs

rules.summary: Mark database-access tests with pytest.mark.db_test.
rules.trigger_intent: writing a test that requires database access
rules.evidence: Use `@pytest.mark.db_test` for tests that require database access.

rules.summary: Place tests in a path mirroring the source path.
rules.trigger_intent: creating a test file
rules.evidence: Test location mirrors source

rules.summary: Do not use caplog; test logic instead of log output.
rules.trigger_intent: writing a test that checks logs
rules.evidence: Do not use `caplog` in tests

rules.summary: Generate reports and scratch output under files/, creating it if needed.
rules.trigger_intent: creating a PR review, report, or scratch-output file
rules.evidence: Put any files you generate (PR reviews, reports, scratch output) under `files/`.

rules.summary: Write commit subjects in imperative plain prose focused on user impact, without Conventional Commit prefixes.
rules.trigger_intent: creating a git commit
rules.evidence: Use the **imperative mood** and a plain message — do **not** use Conventional Commits prefixes

rules.summary: Run prek install before committing code.
rules.trigger_intent: committing code changes
rules.evidence: **Always run `prek install` before committing any code.**

rules.summary: Commit bodies must explain why, never what changed.
rules.trigger_intent: writing a git commit message body
rules.evidence: should describe **why** the change is made — the motivation and context — and **never what** the change is

rules.summary: Never add the agent as a Co-Authored-By commit author.
rules.trigger_intent: creating a git commit message
rules.evidence: NEVER add Co-Authored-By with yourself as co-author of the commit.

rules.summary: Push branches only to origin, never upstream or main.
rules.trigger_intent: pushing a git branch
rules.evidence: Always push branches to `origin`. Never push directly to `upstream` (and never push directly to `main` on either remote).

rules.summary: Before remote commands, inspect remotes and stop for user confirmation if naming is wrong.
rules.trigger_intent: running any command that uses a git remote
rules.evidence: **Before running any remote-based command, run `git remote -v` and verify the names match this convention.**

rules.summary: After changing remotes, inspect them again before continuing.
rules.trigger_intent: continuing after renaming or adding a git remote
rules.evidence: After any rename/add, re-run `git remote -v`

rules.summary: Translate stale remote names to upstream when proposing commands and flag stale docs.
rules.trigger_intent: proposing or running a documented command using a nonstandard remote name
rules.evidence: **translate it to the `upstream` convention**

rules.summary: Before issue work, search for existing PRs and avoid a near-identical new PR.
rules.trigger_intent: starting work on an issue or creating a pull request for it
rules.evidence: Before working on an issue, check for open PRs already addressing it

rules.summary: Before pushing, self-review the full diff and remove unrelated changes.
rules.trigger_intent: pushing a pull request branch
rules.evidence: Review the full diff (`git diff main...HEAD`)

rules.summary: Before pushing, read the code-review instructions and fix every violation.
rules.trigger_intent: pushing a pull request branch
rules.evidence: Read `.github/instructions/code-review.instructions.md` and check your diff against every rule

rules.summary: Before pushing, run and fix fast and manual checks, relevant tests, selective checks, and security review.
rules.trigger_intent: pushing a pull request branch
rules.evidence: Run relevant individual tests and confirm they pass.

rules.summary: Before pushing, rebase onto the latest target branch and ask for guidance if conflicts are too complex.
rules.trigger_intent: pushing a pull request branch
rules.evidence: always rebase your branch onto the latest target branch

rules.summary: Open PR creation in the browser with a prefilled body and checked generative-AI disclosure.
rules.trigger_intent: creating a pull request
rules.evidence: open the PR creation page in the browser with the body pre-filled

rules.summary: PR titles must be under 70 characters, imperative, user-impact focused, and not Conventional Commits.
rules.trigger_intent: writing a pull request title
rules.evidence: keep it short (under 70 chars), in the imperative mood, and focused on user impact

rules.summary: PR bodies need a brief description and applicable issue references.
rules.trigger_intent: writing a pull request body
rules.evidence: Add a brief description of the changes at the top of the body.

rules.summary: When a fix and PR are imminent, do not open a redundant GitHub issue.
rules.trigger_intent: creating a GitHub issue for a problem whose fix will shortly be a PR
rules.evidence: **do not file a GitHub issue first**

rules.summary: For workaround or partial-fix PRs, first create a tracking issue and link it in PR and code.
rules.trigger_intent: creating a pull request containing a workaround, mitigation, version cap, or partial fix
rules.evidence: the deferred work must be captured in a GitHub tracking issue **and** the tracking issue URL must appear as a comment

rules.summary: Use a full tracking-issue URL in the code comment, not a bare issue number.
rules.trigger_intent: adding a code comment at a workaround site
rules.evidence: Use the **full issue URL**, not bare `#NNNNN`

rules.summary: Do not promise a future tracking issue; create and link it before PR submission.
rules.trigger_intent: writing a PR body or code comment for deferred work
rules.evidence: **Do not** write vague forward-looking phrases like "will open a tracking issue"

rules.summary: Tracking issues must describe the workaround, rationale, follow-up work, and removal criteria.
rules.trigger_intent: creating a tracking issue for deferred work
rules.evidence: The tracking issue should describe: what the workaround is, why it was chosen, the concrete follow-up work needed

rules.summary: If an open PR promises a later issue, create it, comment its URL, and add the URL at the workaround site.
rules.trigger_intent: repairing an already-open PR with forward-looking tracking-issue language
rules.evidence: open the tracking issue, add a PR comment referencing the issue URL, and push a follow-up commit

rules.summary: End every agent-drafted GitHub message with the appropriate attribution footer.
rules.trigger_intent: posting an agent-drafted GitHub comment, review, or reply
rules.evidence: must end with an attribution footer

rules.summary: Use the no-human-review footer unless an actual human approved the exact draft.
rules.trigger_intent: posting an agent-drafted GitHub message without prior human approval
rules.evidence: Drafted-by: <Agent Name and Version> (no human review before posting)

rules.summary: Do not tag individuals unless explicitly authorized or replying to active participants.
rules.trigger_intent: posting an agent-authored GitHub message that mentions a person
rules.evidence: AI agents MUST NOT mention or tag individual contributors

rules.summary: Populate the apache-magpie snapshot before invoking its framework skills.
rules.trigger_intent: invoking a pr-management framework skill in a fresh clone
rules.evidence: A fresh clone needs the snapshot populated before any framework skill is invocable.

rules.summary: Never edit the apache-magpie snapshot directly; put adopter changes in overrides.
rules.trigger_intent: modifying an apache-magpie framework workflow
rules.evidence: never edit the snapshot directly

rules.summary: Use magpie-pr-management-code-review for PR reviews, with inline findings unless opted out.
rules.trigger_intent: reviewing a pull request with apache-magpie installed
rules.evidence: use the `magpie-pr-management-code-review` skill for PR code review

rules.summary: Ask before large cross-package refactors, broad-impact dependencies, or destructive data/migrations.
rules.trigger_intent: starting a large cross-package refactor, broad dependency change, or destructive data/migration change
rules.evidence: **Ask first**

rules.summary: Never commit secrets, credentials, or tokens.
rules.trigger_intent: committing changes containing sensitive values
rules.evidence: Commit secrets, credentials, or tokens.

rules.summary: Never hand-edit generated files when a generation workflow exists.
rules.trigger_intent: editing a generated file with an available generation workflow
rules.evidence: Edit generated files by hand when a generation workflow exists.

rules.summary: Never use destructive git operations unless explicitly requested.
rules.trigger_intent: running a destructive git operation
rules.evidence: Use destructive git operations unless explicitly requested.
